sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The DOWN action didn't move the blocks. They're still at cols 29-33, rows 40-41. Looking at the area below:
- Rows 42-44: ccbbbbbGGGGG (blue below blocks, gray to the right)
- Rows 45-48: cccccccccccc (charcoal)
So the blocks can't go DOWN because there's charcoal below.
Let me re-examine the maze structure. The blocks can:
- Go UP from cols 29-33, rows 40-41 → cols 29-33, rows 35-36 (stuck)
- Go RIGHT from cols 29-33, rows 40-41 → cols 34-38, rows 40-41
- Go UP from cols 34-38, rows 40-41 → cols 34-38, rows 5-6 (8 UP actions)
- Go RIGHT from cols 34-38, rows 5-6 → cols 49-53, rows 5-6 (3 RIGHT actions)
- Go DOWN from cols 49-53, rows 5-6 → cols 49-53, rows 20-21 (3 DOWN actions)
- Go UP from cols 49-53, rows 20-21 → cols 49-53, rows 10-11 (2 UP actions) → game over
The game ended after reaching cols 49-53, rows 10-11. But the level was not completed.
Let me check if the blocks can go LEFT from cols 49-53, rows 10-11.
Looking at the area around rows 10-11, cols 49-53:
- The passage at rows 10-11 might be wider than the passage at rows 20-21.
Let me try going UP from cols 29-33, rows 35-36 to see if the blocks can go further up.
Actually, the blocks are at cols 29-33, rows 40-41. Let me try going UP to cols 29-33, rows 35-36, then RIGHT to cols 34-38, rows 35-36, then UP to cols 34-38, rows 30-31, etc.
Wait, but the blocks at cols 29-33, rows 35-36 can't go UP further. Let me check the area above.
Actually, looking at the earlier output:
- Row 33: cccccccGGGGG
- Row 34: cccccccGGGGG
- Row 35: ccOOOOOGGGGG
- Row 36: ccOOOOOGGGGG
So above the blocks (rows 33-34) is charcoal (cccccccGGGGG). The blocks can't go UP from rows 35-36.
But to the right (cols 34-38) is gray (GGGGG). So the blocks can go RIGHT from rows 35-36.
Let me try going RIGHT from cols 29-33, rows 35-36 to cols 34-38, rows 35-36, then UP.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Blocks at cols 29-33, rows 40-41
# Go UP to rows 35-36, then RIGHT to cols 34-38, then UP

# UP 1
result = action(['UP'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"After UP - Orange: {n['boundary']}")
        break

# RIGHT 1
result2 = action(['RIGHT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"After RIGHT - Orange: {n['boundary']}")
        break

# UP 8 (to reach top)
result3 = action(['UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"After 8 UP - Orange: {n['boundary']}")
        break

# RIGHT 3
result4 = action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"After 3 RIGHT - Orange: {n['boundary']}")
        break

# DOWN 3
result5 = action(['DOWN', 'DOWN', 'DOWN'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"After 3 DOWN - Orange: {n['boundary']}")
        break

# LEFT 1 (try to go left from rows 20-21)
result6 = action(['LEFT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"After LEFT - Orange: {n['boundary']}")
        break
print("Game over:", result6.get('game_over', False))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
